title: Ciminalis zollingeri
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant Ciminalis zollingeri. However, I found information about the plant Cimicifuga (now known as Actaea) racemosa, which is sometimes referred to as Actaea zollingeri.

Cimicifuga racemosa, also known as Actaea zollingeri, is a flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, and is believed to help with menopausal symptoms, menstrual cramps, and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cimicifuga racemosa include triterpene glycosides, including actein, cimicifugoside, and notoginsenoside. The most common herbal preparations made with Cimicifuga racemosa include tinctures, capsules, and teas, which are often used to treat symptoms of menopause, menstrual disorders, and anxiety.
